column2: Phase 2 redone around oddkit MCP client; PR #80 closed without merge
column3: Phase 2 redone around oddkit MCP client. Closed PR #80 without merge. New audit-gate.ts uses the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Client to connect to https://oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp via StreamableHTTPClientTransport (already in deps from the AmsMcpAgent work). The runtime calls oddkit_get(input='ams://canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor', knowledge_base_url='https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service') to resolve the persona profile, AND oddkit_get(input=profile.system_prompt_uri, knowledge_base_url=...) to resolve the system prompt URI. Both return content + content_hash. Parsed-profile cache lives in DO state.storage keyed by content_hash per klappy://canon/principles/cache-fetches-and-parses — fetches and parses are both cached against the hash; identical hash means skip both. YAML parsing uses the `yaml` npm dep (~30kb, no transitive deps, MIT), replacing the ~150-line hand-rolled mini-parser from PR #80. Smoke-tested against the merged persona.md: 10/10 sanity checks pass with the real lib.

column2: PR #80 reimplemented three things oddkit gives for free; operator caught it
column3: Operator caught the failure mode with: "How much of this is using oddkit in the codebase? You seem to be redoing some things and handrolling things oddkit gives you for free and deterministicly." PR #80 reimplemented three things oddkit provides: (1) canon URI resolution — used fetch(raw.githubusercontent.com) instead of oddkit_get, bypassing the URI scheme; (2) content fetching with cache validation — no content_hash cache key, every audit re-fetched; (3) YAML profile parsing — ~150 lines hand-rolled. Deeper miss: the persona profile literally declares mcp_servers.operational: [oddkit], meaning the runtime MUST connect to oddkit MCP. Phase 2 v1 built a runtime that didn't. The "Phase 3 wires oddkit MCP" framing was wrong — profile resolution IS what oddkit is for, and it's a Phase 2 concern. The corrected mental model: there are TWO distinct oddkit MCP wirings on the audit-gate path. Runtime → oddkit (Phase 2, this file): resolve persona + system prompt URIs to bootstrap the session. Agent session → oddkit (Phase 3): the agent itself has oddkit wired into its operational tool surface for canon lookups DURING the audit. Phase 2 v1 conflated them and skipped the first one.

column2: When oddkit provides a client capability, the default is to USE IT, not reimplement
column3: When an MCP CLIENT capability (canon URI resolution, content fetching, content-hash caching, YAML/JSON parsing of canonical formats) is provided by oddkit, the default is to USE IT, not reimplement. The bar to reimplement is explicit: substrate-specific code paths (audit-gate's verdict-block extraction is substrate-specific because the output contract format is AMS canon, not oddkit canon) or oddkit gaps. Hand-rolled parsers, raw GitHub fetches against canon URIs, ignored content_hashes, and bypassed URI schemes are all signal-of-failure to use oddkit. Applies to any future agent-runtime, validator, or canon-consumer code in AMS, TruthKit, or sibling repos.

column2: oddkit MCP server is live, reachable from Workers DO, and resolves ams:// via knowledge_base_url override
column3: oddkit MCP server at https://oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp is live and reachable from anywhere with HTTPS. Probed via direct urllib POST: initialize handshake returns server name=oddkit version=0.27.0; tools/call oddkit_get resolves klappy:// URIs directly and ams:// URIs via knowledge_base_url=https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service override. Response shape: MCP SSE single-event, data: {jsonrpc, result: {content: [{type: text, text: '<oddkit-json>'}]}}. The inner oddkit JSON wraps {action, result: {path, content, content_hash}, server_time, debug}. No session id required for these stateless calls (mcp-session-id header was None on init). Workers fetch() can call it from a DO via the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Client + StreamableHTTPClientTransport, both already in worker/package.json dependencies.

column2: Phase 2-v2 PR forthcoming; audit gate auto-fires on code using its own MCP server as client
column3: Phase 2-v2 PR forthcoming. Six files: worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts (rewritten, 617 lines — same size as v1 but ~250 lines of hand-rolled YAML parser replaced with ~50 lines of typed shape assertions on `yaml` lib output; MCP client code is the legitimate substrate addition), worker/wrangler.toml (unchanged from v1 — AUDIT_GATE binding + v4 migration), worker/src/index.ts (unchanged from v1 — import, re-export, POST /audit-gate-test, handleAuditGateTest), worker/src/types.ts (unchanged from v1 — AUDIT_GATE: DurableObjectNamespace), worker/package.json (+1 line for `yaml` dep ^2.6.1), journal/2026-05-12-phase-2-redo-oddkit-mcp-client.tsv (this DOLCHEO trail). Audit gate will auto-fire; expected interaction is the gate auditing the new code that uses the gate's own MCP server as a client. Phase 3 next on operator signal: agent-side Anthropic + agent-side oddkit MCP wiring, endpoint auth, side-by-side validation.
